Chapter 4: The map

This is the map of the walls of Attack on Titan, inside which we geolocalized the items of our Collection. You can explore the map and zoom in wherever you like, discover some interesting information about the world architecture of Attack on Titan and the appearance of the items. To know more about them you can click or tap on the white circles in the map.

Location Details

More information about Wall Maria.

Location Details

More information about Wall Rose.

Location Details: Wall Sina

More information about Wall Sina. The King resides here.

Location Details: The Walls

In the anime, a "Currently Publicly Available Information" snippet reveals the area left to humans and how far it extends.
The walls of Attack on Titan are inspired by the existing city of Nördlingen situated in Germany. The round circle enclosing the city is depicting the walls enclosing humanity.

Learn more about the Walls.

Location Details: Stohess District, the Baptistery

The Stohess District is a calm and wealthy city attached to Wall Sina. The Survey Corps decided to use this location to bait and imprison a Titan after discovering its user's identity. The capture encounters hardships and Eren is forced to fight the Female Titan: in this scene we see a Baptistery that is used by the followers of the Cult of the Walls.
The architecture of the Baptistery in Attack on Titan is the same as the one we can see in Pisa, Italy: Battistero di San Giovanni. Many buildings' architectural structures are reminiscing european's roman and gothic style.

Learn more about the Stohess District and the raid.

Location Details: Trost District, Cannons

The Trost District is the southern-most city of Wall Maria and second afflicted area by an attack of Titans.
After five years from the first attack, the Colossal Titan returns and breaches Wall Rose; at this moment, important discoveries about the Titans are surfacing.
Here you can find one of the many examples of the Anti-Titan artillery. The cannons sitting atop the walls here weren't always efficient nor mounted this way to kill Titans, but the very interesting part about this technology lies in the fact that the further one explores the story the more one gets to know how the cannons of the Walls weren't the only ones existing for the same purpose...
The resemblance is close to the documentation of 19th century cannons.

Learn more about the Trost District, Wall-mounted artillery and Anti-Titan Artillery.

Location Details: Flying Machine

In the very first moments of the narration, we are introduced to a corp of soldiers fighting in the woods - the Survey Corps. While riding their horses, some of them start to take flight themeselves with the help of a machinery attached to their waists: the ODM gear (Omin-directional mobility gear). It's a ssystem of tightly fitted belts around the upper and lower body which act as compensation for the pulling force when the soldiers move. Witht the help of specialized metal arrows they move like monkeys swinging among trees, and aim the swords at their sides to kill the Titans.
A similar vision takes place in Leonardo Da Vinci's creation: the Flying Machine. With which he imagined single individuals to be able to fly through the help of a helix pushing air against the ground.

Learn more about the Survey Corps and the ODM Gear.

Location Details: Shiganshina District

The Shiganshina District is were the narration starts. That day where everything changed for humanity and the life of the three child protagonists, that day when "peace" was ruined after so many years... here, a lot of key turning points of the plot take place. Click or tap on the button below the have all the information about the items of Shiganshina.

Learn more about Shiganshina District.

Enter Shiganshina District

Location Details: The Outside

What if your whole life ever consisted of being caged inside 50-meter high constraints made of concrete ?
What if all you ever knew about "outside" is through reading about it in books and dreaming of vast lands made of salty water or of "fiery water" which you know you will never have the chance to see ?
What if the fragile balance of the world was suddenly disrupted one day only for you to be reminded of the effimeracy of life and the fleeting illusion of "freedom" ?
And then, you fight for your right to live because you are born in this world only to discover answers made of greyscale truths ?

Enter the Outside
